svg.text: SOMALI
HISTORY & HORN OF AFRICA
SOMALI HISTORY
Ancient Land of Punt: Egypt traded with Somalia ~2500 BCE
7th-10th c. CE: Arab traders arrive; Islam adopted
Ajuran Sultanate (13th-17th c.): hydraulic engineering, resistance
Sultanate of the Geledi • Majeerteen Sultanate (19th c.)
1960: Independence from Britain + Italy; unified Somali Republic
1972: Latin script adopted for Somali — mass literacy campaign
SOMALI TODAY
~20M speakers; official in Somalia and Somaliland
Somali diaspora: largest in UK, USA, Scandinavia, Gulf states
Ancient oral poetry tradition: gabay (long odes) — living art form
OROMO HISTORY · SECONDARY LANGUAGE
GADAA SYSTEM — OROMO DEMOCRACY
Gadaa: indigenous Oromo democratic governance system
Grade-based government: men advance through 8-year grades
Irreecha: thanksgiving festival — largest Oromo annual gathering
UNESCO: Gadaa recognized as Intangible Cultural Heritage (2016)
OROMO EXPANSION & SUPPRESSION
16th c. Oromo migration expanded across Ethiopian highlands
Abyssinian empire: Oromo language suppressed for centuries
Menelik II conquest: Oromo lands absorbed into Ethiopia (1880s)
1974-1991: Derg regime bans Oromo language in schools
1991: EPRDF victory; Oromo gains federal regional recognition
OROMO TODAY
~40M speakers — largest ethnic group in Ethiopia
Oromia region: official language in largest Ethiopian state
Qubee Latin script: politically charged symbol of Oromo identity
CUSHITIC PEOPLES OF THE HORN
Somali and Oromo: the two largest Cushitic-speaking peoples
Proto-Cushitic homeland: Horn of Africa ~3000 BCE
Shared geography: both shaped by Rift Valley ecology
Both: pastoral nomadic tradition • cattle = wealth in both cultures
Both: Islam as majority religion (different arrival paths)
Two great Cushitic civilizations of the same ancient horn
PLATE 32d ALT · SOMALI / OROMO · HISTORY
